label: Causal Attention
definition: Self-attention with a mask that forbids each position from attending to future tokens, enforcing the left-to-right constraint of generation. Its quiet payoff is efficiency: because no position can peek at the answer, you can train next-token prediction on every position of a whole sequence in one parallel pass, and it is the line dividing a decoder-only LLM from a bidirectional encoder like BERT.

body.text: Causal attention is ordinary self-attention with one rule added: a token may attend to itself and to earlier tokens, but never to later ones. This is implemented with a mask that zeroes out the attention weights to future positions before the softmax, so information can only flow backward in the sequence. The "causal" name reflects that the present cannot depend on the future.

This constraint is what makes autoregressive language modeling work, and the payoff is efficiency. The goal is to predict each next token from only the tokens before it, and during training you want to do that for every position in the sequence simultaneously. Causal masking makes that safe: even though the whole sequence is processed in one parallel pass, no position can cheat by looking at the answer, the tokens that come after it. Without the mask, the model would trivially see the token it is supposed to predict, and training would learn nothing.

It is the defining difference between a decoder-only language model and a bidirectional encoder like BERT, which uses unmasked attention so every token sees the entire input, appropriate for understanding tasks but not for generation. The choice of mask is, in effect, the choice between a model that reads and one that writes.

The causal structure also has a useful consequence at inference: because a token's attention never depends on future tokens, the keys and values of past tokens are fixed once computed, which is exactly what the kv-cache stores and reuses to make generation efficient. The same property that makes parallel training safe is what makes incremental generation cheap.

image.alt: Block diagram of scaled dot-product attention flowing upward: queries and keys enter a matrix multiply, then a scale step, then a mask step, then softmax to produce attention scores, which a final matrix multiply combines with the values to give the context vector.
image.caption: Causal attention is scaled dot-product attention with the mask step shown here doing the work. After the query-key scores are scaled, the mask sets every score that points to a future token to negative infinity, so the following softmax gives those positions zero weight. Each position can then attend only to itself and earlier tokens, which is what lets a model be trained on next-token prediction over a whole sequence at once.
